methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GalaxEye is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Suyash Singh and Kishan Thakkar that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 4 private peers at same or adjacent stage (ClearSpace at $400M Series A; Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B Series B; Aalyria at $1.3B Series B; Hubble Network at $400M Series B) and 5 public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $20M based on the $10M raised proxy. Peer-median valuation anchors to the $850M median across the 4 identified private comps, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data. The private peer set exhibits wide dispersion, reflecting heterogeneity in space-sector funding and stage.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $17M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $435M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($20M) and peer-median ($850M) reflects tension between asset-based reasoning and market comparables. Key uncertainties include GalaxEye's operational maturity relative to the peer set, revenue trajectory, and whether peers' valuations reflect genuine market consensus or funding-environment exuberance. Revenue disclosure would enable public-comp anchoring as a third cross-check.
